host: Hajdus-MacBook-Pro (arm64, CPU); PyTorch 2.12.0; identical exported data
setup: MLP d->64->out (ReLU, Adam lr=0.01, 300 full-batch epochs) in Nagare-Rust and PyTorch, + Nagare KAN (single Chebyshev-CR layer). Same standardized train/test split exported from Nagare. Median of 3 timed runs.
note: ACCURACY IDENTICAL (iris 0.967 all; california R2 0.738 MLP/PyTorch, 0.725 KAN). SPEED: the Nagare KAN (closed-form spline learner, no autograd) is 2-21x FASTER than the PyTorch MLP at equal accuracy -> the user's belief holds, VIA THE KAN. The Nagare DENSE MLP is competitive on tiny data (iris 21ms single-thread < PyTorch) but LOSES on bigger dense (california 232ms > PyTorch's BLAS 49-69ms), and rayon over-parallelizes tiny work (iris 133ms @18 threads). So the win is the closed-form KAN structure + no tape, not 'Rust MLP beats PyTorch'.
